maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.subtitle: Manage defects and inspections
maintenance.defectManagementDescription: Create defect reports, track repairs, and update statuses
maintenance.inspectionDescription: Manage inspection intervals and review overdue inspections
maintenance.inspectionsSubtitle: Manage inspection intervals and upcoming inspections

eventory.title: Eventory Integration
eventory.subtitle: Connect to Eventory to import rental products automatically
eventory.apiUrlDesc: The base URL of the Eventory API, e.g. https://api.eventory.se
eventory.passwordPlaceholderUpdate: Enter a new password to replace the existing one
eventory.credentialsDesc: Enter username and password to authenticate via OAuth2 password grant. Leave password blank to keep the existing one; use Clear to revoke it.
eventory.apiKeyDesc: Use an API key instead of username/password. Leave blank to keep the existing key; use Clear to revoke it.
eventory.keyPlaceholderUpdate: Enter a new key to replace the existing one
eventory.tokenEndpointDesc: OAuth2 token URL. Leave blank to use the default /oauth/token path.
eventory.supplierNameDesc: The supplier name assigned to imported products. Defaults to 'Eventory' if left blank.
eventory.syncIntervalDesc: How often to automatically sync products from Eventory in the background.
eventory.priceMarginDesc: Optional percentage markup applied to the Eventory price to compute the customer price on sync. Set to 0 to disable automatic customer price calculation.
eventory.actionsDesc: Fetch a preview of products from Eventory, or sync them directly into your rental products list.
eventory.actionsUnsavedHint: Save your settings before fetching or syncing.
eventory.fetchError: Failed to fetch products from Eventory
eventory.syncError: Failed to sync products from Eventory
eventory.syncNote: Synced products are saved under supplier name "{{supplier}}" in the Rental Products list.
eventory.noProducts: No products returned by the Eventory API.
eventory.info1: Products are imported into the Rental Products table under the configured supplier name.
eventory.info2: Re-running Sync updates existing products and adds new ones.
eventory.info3: Your API key and password are stored in the database and never exposed in full in the interface.
eventory.credentialKeyDesc: The credential key encrypts the API key and password stored in the database. Using the EVENTORY_CREDENTIAL_KEY environment variable is recommended for production — the database value is a convenient fallback for environments where setting environment variables is impractical.
eventory.credentialKeyEnvActive: Key is active via environment variable (EVENTORY_CREDENTIAL_KEY). The database value is ignored.
eventory.credentialKeyEnvInvalid: Environment variable EVENTORY_CREDENTIAL_KEY is set but contains an invalid key (wrong length or encoding). Encryption will fail until this is corrected.
eventory.credentialKeyDbActive: Key is active via database setting.
eventory.credentialKeyDbInvalid: A key is stored in the database but it is invalid (wrong length or encoding). Encryption will fail until the key is replaced.
eventory.credentialKeyNotSet: No key configured. Newly saved credentials will be stored as plain text, and previously encrypted credentials may be unreadable without the original key.
eventory.credentialKeyPlaceholder: Paste a base64-encoded 32-byte key, or use Generate
eventory.keyGenerated: New key generated. Copy it and save it securely before saving — it will not be shown again.
eventory.keyGeneratedSaved: New key generated and saved to the database.
eventory.keyError: Failed to update the encryption key.
eventory.keyWarning: If you change or clear the key, previously encrypted credentials will no longer be readable. You will need to re-enter them.

currencySettings.title: Currency Settings
currencySettings.subtitle: Configure the currency symbol displayed throughout the application
currencySettings.currencySymbolDesc: The symbol shown next to monetary values (e.g. prices, costs).
currencySettings.examples: Examples: € (Euro), $ (US Dollar), £ (Pound), Fr. (Swiss Franc), SEK, kr
currencySettings.note1: The currency symbol is only a display label and does not affect how prices are stored or calculated.
currencySettings.note2: Changes apply after saving and will appear on pages when they are refreshed or revisited.
currencySettings.symbolTooLong: Currency symbol must be 8 characters or fewer

apiSettings.title: API Limits
apiSettings.subtitle: Configure the maximum number of devices and cases loaded by the API
apiSettings.deviceLimitDesc: Maximum number of devices loaded when accessing the label page and other requests.
apiSettings.caseLimitDesc: Maximum number of cases loaded when accessing the label page and other requests.
apiSettings.recommended: Recommended: 50000 for normal use, higher for very large inventories
apiSettings.recommendedCases: Recommended: 50000 for normal use, higher for many cases
apiSettings.note1: Very high limits may impact performance
apiSettings.note2: The limits apply only to API requests, not to the database
apiSettings.note3: Changes take effect immediately, no restart required
apiSettings.note4: With very large datasets, loading may take longer

export.title: CSV Export
export.subtitle: Export different datasets as CSV files for Excel or other applications. All exports use UTF-8 encoding and German CSV format (semicolon separated).

notes.title: Important Notes for CSV Export
notes.encodingText: All CSV files use UTF-8 with BOM for optimal Excel compatibility
notes.delimiterText: Semicolon (;) as column separator (German CSV format)
notes.excelImportText: Files can be opened directly in Excel. If there are issues, use "Data → From Text/CSV"

jobs.label: All Jobs
jobs.description: Jobs with date, customer, status, and device count

ledSettings.title: LED Behavior
ledSettings.subtitle: Configure the appearance and behavior of LED lighting
ledSettings.singleBinSubtitle: Default appearance for the "Highlight bin" function
ledSettings.previewBinHint: Leave empty to automatically use the first bin from mapping.
ledSettings.jobSubtitle: Configure LED appearance in job visualization
ledSettings.jobModeHintAllBins: All bins light up: required bins stand out via different colors/patterns.
ledSettings.jobModeHintRequiredOnly: Only bins with devices still to pack light up; all others are switched off.
ledSettings.requiredBinsDescription: Used when devices still need to be packed in a bin.
ledSettings.nonRequiredBinsDescription: Bins not needed for the current job.
ledSettings.mappingSubtitle: Assign LED pixels to individual areas (e.g. bins, grid boxes). Configuration is persisted in the container and survives deployments.
ledSettings.mappingLoadFailed: Mapping could not be loaded. Check server logs.
ledSettings.defaultAppearanceHint: Define the default appearance for LED highlights in mapping
ledSettings.noGroups: No groups created yet. Add one via "Add group".
ledSettings.groupIdentifier: Group identifier (e.g. Shelf A, Grid box 1)
ledSettings.zoneTypeSubtitle: Adjust LED behavior for individual zone types

messages.previewStartedFor: ✓ Preview for {{target}} started - "Stop preview" ends lighting.
